report_metadata.investigation_target: Subito.it impersonation phishing — phone +39 350 860 6411 / domain verifieer.cc
report_metadata.investigator_tooling: Windows CLI (curl, nslookup, openssl) + free keyless public APIs (RDAP, urlscan.io) + web search
report_metadata.evidence_integrity_note: All values below are copied from live tool output captured at the time above. No value is inferred or fabricated. Raw responses are stored verbatim in ./raw/. Fields that could not be obtained (paid/key-gated services) are explicitly marked status=NOT_QUERIED.

number_range_type.value: Italian mobile range (3xx). 350 is an allocated mobile prefix.
number_range_type.note: Specific carrier CANNOT be reliably derived from the prefix because of Italian mobile number portability (MNP). Authoritative carrier requires a live HLR lookup.

carrier_line_type_region.reason: NumVerify / apilayer / HLR-lookup free tiers all require a registered API key, which is not available in this environment. No result was obtained; none is invented.
carrier_line_type_region.how_to_complete: Register a free NumVerify key and run: Invoke-RestMethod "http://apilayer.net/api/validate?access_key=KEY&number=393508606411&country_code=IT"

breach_database_presence.reason: HaveIBeenPwned phone/email API and equivalents are key-gated and paid. Not queried; no result fabricated.
breach_database_presence.how_to_complete: Use HIBP API (paid key) or check the number against locally-held breach corpora only if lawfully obtained.

truecaller_lookup.reason: Truecaller has no free/legal public API; scraping it violates its ToS and may be unlawful. Deliberately not attempted.

public_scam_db_web_search.value: No public report found tying this exact number to a scam as of capture time.
public_scam_db_web_search.interpretation: Absence is consistent with a WhatsApp Business number registered ~1 month ago: report aggregators (stopscam.it, numfinder, whuis, punto-informatico spam lists) lag fresh scammer numbers. Absence is NOT exoneration.
recommended_manual_checks: Telegram @ScamAlertBot / community scam channels (requires Telegram account; cannot be queried from CLI)

2_whatsapp_business_analysis.claim: Number used a WhatsApp Business account registered 'last month'.

registration_check.reason: There is no official/legal API to confirm WhatsApp registration of an arbitrary number. Unofficial 'is-on-whatsapp' endpoints abuse the WA protocol and are unreliable/ToS-violating; not used.
registration_check.lawful_alternative: Open a normal WhatsApp chat to the number from a handset and screenshot the presence of a 'Business' badge and account-created indicators as victim evidence.

social_engineering_indicator.value: Use of WhatsApp BUSINESS (vs personal) is a recognized trust-boost tactic: the green/verified-style business label lends false legitimacy. Combined with a recently-registered number it fits the throwaway-infrastructure pattern.
social_engineering_indicator.confidence: MEDIUM (behavioural inference, not a measured datum)

temporal_correlation.phishing_subdomain_first_seen: subito.verifieer.cc first captured by urlscan 2026-06-06; urlscan reports the subdomain age ~30 days at capture (≈ early May 2026)
temporal_correlation.assessment: WhatsApp registration window and the stand-up of the subito.* phishing subdomain align to early May 2026 — coherent single-campaign timeline.

registrant_identity.value: Not disclosed in RDAP (no registrant vCard present; .cc registry + privacy). Apex sits behind Cloudflare, masking origin.

registration_vs_activation_gap.note: Apex registered 2026-02-03 but the subito.* phishing host and its TLS cert were stood up ~2026-06-01 (cert notBefore) / first urlscan 2026-06-06. The domain was aged ~4 months before weaponisation against Subito — a common 'aged-domain' evasion tactic.

live_phishing_capture.interpretation: Live page impersonating SUBITO.IT with an Italian fake listing ('Seggiolino per bicicletta' = child bike seat). Status 200 = active phishing at capture.

earliest_capture.note: Apex flagged 'possiblethreat' by a community submitter on registration day.

tls_certificate.note: Free 90-day Google Trust Services DV cert issued 2026-06-01 — matches the early-June weaponisation window.

virustotal.reason: VirusTotal v3 API requires an API key (x-apikey header). No key in environment; result not fabricated.

securitytrails_dns_history.reason: SecurityTrails requires an API key. Passive-DNS history not retrieved.

resolved_ips_subito_verifieer_cc.note: Identical IP set to apex — both are Cloudflare anycast edge addresses, NOT the true origin server.

ip_whois.org: Cloudflare, Inc., 101 Townsend Street, San Francisco, CA 94107, US

origin_server.value: HIDDEN behind Cloudflare reverse proxy. True hosting IP/provider is NOT observable via DNS.
origin_server.how_to_obtain: Only Cloudflare (via abuse report / legal-disclosure request) or the .cc registry/registrar can reveal origin. This is why the abuse report to Cloudflare plus escalation through the relevant national cybercrime channel is the correct path.

http_server_fingerprint.root_status: 404 (phishing served only on deep /a/<token> paths, root returns 404 — classic targeted-link phishing)

abuseipdb.reason: AbuseIPDB API requires a free API key (Key header). Not queried; no score invented.
abuseipdb.caveat: Even if queried, the IPs are shared Cloudflare edge IPs — an AbuseIPDB score would reflect Cloudflare in aggregate, not this actor. Low evidentiary value.

confidence_legend.HIGH: Directly observed in registry/DNS/TLS/HTTP tool output captured this session.
confidence_legend.MEDIUM: Inference or correlation from observed data + user-reported facts; plausible, not proven.
confidence_legend.NOT_QUERIED: Service requires a paid/key-gated API not available here; deliberately left blank rather than fabricated.
